findings.signature: Eight specific defaults chosen for remaining P1 remediation: auto modes remove waits, /memory:* stays markdown-only, /memory:save plan-only-by-default, embedding-cache rows deleted on retention delete, memory_save planner inputs internal-only, governed-ingest validation extends to ingest paths, args validated before pre-dispatch logic, legacy strict-warning packet grandfathered
findings.reuse: These defaults are release-gate constraints that any future planner must honor; they shape the memory_save MCP schema, /memory:* command architecture, and ingest governance contract.
findings.notes: A /speckit:plan author touching memory_save, /memory:*, or ingest governance needs these defaults to avoid re-litigating decisions already made under release-readiness pressure.

findings.signature: PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 is the controlling license for external-project patterns; the actual shipped mitigation was an upstream-project name scrub (commit bdd3c831d) not a verbatim license quote; P0 governance gate blocking all 012 code sub-phases; .gitignore excludes external/ so worktrees lack the LICENSE file
findings.reuse: Any future code touching external-project patterns must honor the clean-room allow-list and name-scrub posture documented in the ADR; verbatim-code reuse remains forbidden.
findings.notes: The spec's status-note documents a material change in mitigation strategy (name scrub vs verbatim quote) that the decision-record ADR captures. A planner must know the current posture, not the historical one.

findings.signature: Two-step pipeline: generator scaffolds structure and output path, Sonnet agent rewrites Summary/Added/Changed/Fixed/Files-Changed/Prune-FollowUps using a written contract; 441 phases need HVR-voice changelogs; fabrication guard plus adversarial verify; flat-per-track layout with 696 final files (624 leaf + 72 rollups + 2 indexes)
findings.reuse: The changelog generator at scripts/changelog/ and the enrichment contract are reusable infrastructure for any future packet-local changelog generation.
findings.notes: Operationally important: the root rollup index files in tracks 002/006/007 were NOT refreshed after the flat-per-track flatten (review-changelog-completeness/review-report.md:95). They still link to old nested directory shapes and under-report. A regeneration pass is needed.

findings.signature: Divergent signal-handler stacks (context-server.ts:1681-1692 exit 0 vs shutdown-hooks.ts:129-148 exit 143/130) race under concurrent-session SIGTERM; if shutdown-hooks stack wins it skips fatalShutdown ordered drain, coupling unfenced ingest worker dirty-WAL with non-re--entrant socket server; operationally reachable with 2 Claude + 4 codex sessions sharing one repo
findings.reuse: This compound failure analysis is the justification for the single-ordered-teardown fix in 017-last-50-commits-review-remediation; any future shutdown-path change must preserve the ordering.
findings.notes: The 017 remediation landed a single ordered teardown, but the compound-failure analysis documents exactly which code paths interact and why ordering matters. A planner modifying shutdown hooks must read this.

findings.signature: Per-track ROOT ROLLUP index files not refreshed after 026 changelog tree was flattened to one-subdir-level / flat-files-per-track; they still link to old nested directory shapes and stop at pre-flatten dates, under-reporting and mis-linking the (complete, present) leaf changelogs
findings.reuse: A single regeneration pass over the *-root.md rollups in tracks 002/006/007 would clear this drift.
findings.notes: Concrete documentation gap: the leaf layer is healthy but the index layer drifted. An operator reading the root rollups sees incomplete coverage.

findings.signature: getGraphReadinessSnapshot and getGraphFreshness are documented/contractual read-only but call detectState which writes last_git_head to DB, violating ADR-003 single-writer discipline; function docstrings and status.ts inline comment claim no side effects; P1 idempotent non-corrupting
findings.reuse: Fix: move setLastGitHead out of detectState into ensureCodeGraphReady only, or pass mutate:false. The proposed fix is sound and documented.
findings.notes: A /speckit:plan author touching code-graph status/readiness must know this contract violation exists. The write is idempotent (SQLite serializes, same value) so it's non-corrupting, but it makes the read-only promise false.

findings.signature: daemon-recycle-transparency-stress.vitest.ts uses a local replay loop copy rather than the production replaySnapshot() closure in launcher-session-proxy.cjs:595; cannot catch drift in the production closure
findings.reuse: The stress test should drive an exported production replay helper or a real proxy recycle path; currently it validates the classifyFrame logic but not the production closure.
findings.notes: P1 test-fidelity finding from the 014 deep review. Downgrade only if the test drives an exported production replay helper.

findings.signature: OpenCode code-graph plugin bridge (mk-code-graph-bridge.mjs) is non-functional by deliberate choice; import fix was attempted, smoke-verified, then REVERTED because the runnable bridge calls initializeDb()/sessionManager.init() directly against daemon-owned memory DB (dual-writer hazard); root fix deferred to IPC-backed transport in 028 code-index runtime-integration phase
findings.reuse: The bridge stays inert; the README documents the non-functional state. Any planner must know the OpenCode code-graph integration path requires 028 to ship first.
findings.notes: This is a genuine open gap: the OpenCode code-graph integration is blocked on 028's IPC-backed transport. The bridge's dual-writer hazard was caught by fresh-model review after mechanical verification passed.
